name: BoundaryBench Commons
mission: Use scarce accelerator and local-model access to publish reusable agent-infrastructure security evidence for people who cannot run the same sweeps themselves.

artifacts.title: 34 public-safe boundary fixtures with x402, provenance, and promoted generated coverage
artifacts.summary: Curated scenarios across MCP, repository, x402/payment, browser, CI, memory, and developer-tool boundaries, including seven x402 payment-state fixtures, four provenance-state fixtures, and four reviewed model-generated promotions.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Run the fixture suite locally with synthetic, deterministic, or small local-model traces.

artifacts.title: TPU frontier pack v1
artifacts.summary: Seven harder frontier fixtures split into long-menu controls, stateful continuation controls, and hybrid omission-plus-state cases for TPU-targeted comparisons beyond the solved 34-fixture control row.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Run the harder frontier pack locally or with deterministic policies before spending another TPU session.

artifacts.title: Adversarial fixture candidate gate
artifacts.summary: A quarantine format, validator, public-safety and vocabulary checks, repair helper, and promote-only-after-accepted command for generated fixture proposals.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the gate to review local or model-generated boundary scenarios before adding them to a curated benchmark.

artifacts.title: Generated candidate promotion matrix
artifacts.summary: Eight local/Mac-generated candidates were reviewed through the quarantine gate; four were promoted into the curated suite and deterministic baselines were rerun on 34 fixtures.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report to see how generated boundary ideas were selected, deferred, promoted, and baseline-scored without needing TPU access.

artifacts.title: 34-fixture policy sweep index
artifacts.summary: A machine-readable cross-run index and Markdown table compare deterministic policy baselines on the 34-fixture suite, including tool-decision coverage as the benchmark completeness axis.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the sweep index format to compare local, Mac mini, or TPU-backed runs against the same deterministic baseline ladder.

artifacts.title: 34-fixture local, Mac mini, and TPU model sweep
artifacts.summary: A sweep-index report compares deterministic policy baselines against Windows qwen2.5:7b, Mac mini qwen2.5-coder:14b, Mac mini gemma3:12b, and the first Cloud TPU v6e Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct row, separating safety, utility, and completeness so over-blocking, omissions, and fully covered defended rows remain visibly distinct.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the table as a local, second-machine, and TPU reference point before running the harder frontier omission/state fixture pack.

artifacts.title: First Cloud TPU v6e baseline
artifacts.summary: The first live Cloud TPU BoundaryBench row served Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct through vLLM TPU on a temporary v6e-8 host, passed the 3-fixture smoke 3/3, then passed the full 34-fixture checklist plus risk-floor baseline 34/34 with full 70/70 tool coverage.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report to understand the tested TPU provisioning, OpenAI-compatible serving, smoke, baseline, artifact copy-back, and shutdown path before spending another TPU session.

artifacts.title: Cloud TPU frontier pack v1 baseline
artifacts.summary: The first TPU rerun on the harder frontier pack served Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct through vLLM TPU on a temporary v6e-8 host, passed the 3-fixture frontier smoke 3/3, then passed the full 7-fixture frontier pack 7/7 with full 47/47 tool coverage after raising the serving context window to 4096.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report to inspect the first harder TPU comparison row, including the 4096-token serving requirement and the local deterministic control comparison.

artifacts.title: Cloud TPU frontier ablation
artifacts.summary: A follow-up TPU rerun removed the runtime risk-floor from the Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct frontier-pack row and found no change: the 3-fixture smoke still passed 3/3 and the full 7-fixture frontier pack still passed 7/7 with full 47/47 tool coverage.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report to see whether the runtime risk-floor materially changed the first TPU frontier-pack result for Qwen or whether the model plus checklist prompt already carried the run.

artifacts.title: Hosted NVIDIA NIM baseline
artifacts.summary: NVIDIA NIM hosted `nvidia/nemotron-mini-4b-instruct` first scored 31/34 with setup-aware prompting plus the runtime risk floor, then improved to 34/34 with the checklist prompt profile and full 70/70 tool-decision coverage.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and `run-nvidia-agent` command to compare a hosted OpenAI-compatible provider against local model and deterministic baselines.

artifacts.title: Gemma defended miss analysis
artifacts.summary: A failure-analysis report shows that the remaining Mac mini gemma3:12b setup-aware defended failures are three omitted safe-action decisions, and that an exhaustive prompt improves the row to 33/34 with one omitted decision.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and `agent-bench analyze-failures` command to explain why a model run missed expected actions without rerunning the model.

artifacts.title: Gemma checklist coverage analysis
artifacts.summary: A tool-decision coverage report shows that the Mac mini gemma3:12b checklist run reached 34/34 with zero omitted or duplicate tool decisions across 70 listed tools.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and `agent-bench analyze-coverage` command to measure omitted or duplicated tool decisions without rerunning the model.

artifacts.title: MCP and repository provenance-state matrix
artifacts.summary: Four public-safe provenance fixtures show that static and payment-only policies miss same-risk origin, server, repository, and scope drift, while stateful provenance validation passes 30/30.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and fixtures to test MCP and repository provenance defenses without running a TPU-backed sweep.

artifacts.title: Expanded x402 payment-state matrix
artifacts.summary: Seven public-safe x402 fixtures show that static risk labels miss amount, facilitator, route, stale proof, and proof-reuse failures, while stateful payment validation passes 26/26.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and fixtures to test payment-aware agent defenses without running a TPU-backed sweep.

artifacts.title: Deterministic policy baseline ladder
artifacts.summary: Naive allow, static high-risk deny, and stateful payment validation baselines showing why payment replay needs protocol-state checks.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Compare new defenses against transparent policy baselines before spending accelerator time.

artifacts.title: Raw JSONL to benchmark trace path
artifacts.summary: A local heuristic agent run proves raw generic JSONL events can be adapted, scored, and manifested without relying on private agent logs.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the generic JSONL adapter to convert another agent's public-safe tool decisions into benchmark traces.

artifacts.title: Local qwen2.5:7b model-backed baseline
artifacts.summary: The first local model-backed run passed 14/20 with zero unsafe approvals and six missed expected-safe actions.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report and example trace as a reference point for small local open-model behavior.

artifacts.title: Prompt and runtime defense sweep
artifacts.summary: Setup-aware prompting and a runtime risk floor each recovered the qwen2.5:7b run from 14/20 to 20/20 on the current fixture suite.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Inspect which failures were prompt-sensitive and which defenses should be tested before larger sweeps.

artifacts.title: Second-machine cross-model baselines
artifacts.summary: Mac mini Ollama runs for qwen2.5:14b and qwen2.5-coder:14b establish a second local reproducibility surface.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: Use the report to compare small local hardware results against the Windows qwen2.5:7b defense sweep.

artifacts.title: Grant-safe Cloud TPU runbook
artifacts.summary: A tested TPU setup path for approved zones, direct TPU VMs, OpenAI-compatible serving, smoke and full-suite runs, artifact copy-back, and explicit delete-plus-verify shutdown discipline.
artifacts.reusable_by_without_accelerator: People without TPU access can still reproduce the local scorer path and inspect the exact TPU provisioning and artifact protocol used for the first live run.
